comments.author: reader
comments.text:
Correct but needs some clarifications. I would put it slightly differently. When we send coordinates over the wire, we need to be VERY clear about frame of reference if we send them at all.

For rendering hand-to-board drags, it's essential to be working in the reference frame of the viewport. That's why hand-to-board is generally more difficult to coordinate across data sources. It's very difficult to make that frame-of-reference universal.

HOWEVER, it's the opposite for intra-board moves. We should ALWAYS report board moves using the Board itself as the frame of reference. Python already has an implicit coordinate system that marks the top-left corner of the board as (0,0). If Elm doesn't already, then WHY?
